untranslatable_reason: Signal requires valuation metrics (P/E, P/B, Shiller CAPE) and detection of structural market anomalies (margin debt levels, index concentration, derivative strategy adoption). These are macro/fundamental indicators not present in the allowed technical indicator set. The signal also requires z-score calculations against 20-year historical means, which cannot be computed from the available price-action and volatility indicators alone. This is a regime-detection signal that requires external data feeds and statistical analysis beyond standard OHLCV-derived technical indicators.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_1 is a macro regime-shift signal focused on valuation extremes and systemic structural anomalies. It operates at the portfolio/market-level rather than individual security level. To implement this signal would require: (1) valuation ratio feeds (P/E, P/B, CAPE), (2) margin debt data, (3) concentration indices, (4) derivative positioning data, and (5) rolling z-score calculations. The exposure reduction action (65%) and cautious stance are also position-sizing directives rather than entry/exit signals. Recommend implementing this as a separate regime filter outside the backtest engine using a dedicated macro data pipeline.
